beats.activity: Wake up, coffee ritual while watching the light come over the water.
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit; creature of routine. Yesterday's concert energy still high but tax season resumes today. Start with grounding ritual before dealing with transit strike logistics.

beats.activity: Drive to First Hill office; transit strike means no Link, so car is only option. Rideshare surge pricing makes driving own vehicle sensible.
beats.reasoning: Metro strike until 4pm forces alternative commute. Felix is practical; he'll drive rather than pay surge pricing. Morning commute window avoids peak traffic.

beats.activity: Tax season grind: client meetings, reconciliations, spreadsheets. Mid-morning water break to stay hydrated.
beats.reasoning: June is peak tax season; Felix is in the thick of it. Mild weather doesn't interrupt office work, but hydration matters after yesterday's heat. No sign he's flagging on energy yet.

beats.activity: Lunch from food truck on Cherry Street; sits in shade and people-watches.
beats.reasoning: Felix knows every food truck rotation and trusts them. Yesterday he grabbed the same lunch spot; it's a habit. Mild 68F weather is perfect for outdoor eating without yesterday's brutality.

beats.activity: Back to office for afternoon tax work: follow-ups, client calls, final reconciliations before end of day.
beats.reasoning: Tax season doesn't pause. Felix powers through the afternoon. By 4pm the transit strike ends, but he'll drive home regardless since he came by car.

beats.activity: Drive home to Madrona; light traffic post-strike as transit comes back online.
beats.reasoning: End-of-day commute. Transit strike impact fading. Felix heads home early enough to have evening to himself.

beats.activity: Quick shower and light dinner at home; something simple.
beats.reasoning: Decompression after tax day. Felix values simple meals at home to reset. No big plans yet—energy is moderate after yesterday's concert high.

beats.activity: Evening at pottery studio; centering clay, making something shapeless whole.
beats.reasoning: Felix has been to the wheel twice in the last three days—it's his anchor activity after intense work. Mild evening weather supports the short drive to Capitol Hill. Hands need that motion.

beats.activity: Drive back to Madrona; wind down for the night.
beats.reasoning: Return from pottery before late hour. Felix respects his early-riser schedule and needs wind-down time before bed.

diary:
Started the day the way I like it—coffee on the porch watching the water turn gold. The transit strike meant I had to drive in, which honestly wasn't the worst thing. Gave me time to think before diving into the tax grind.

First Hill was the usual chaos of reconciliations and client calls. Grabbed lunch from the food truck on Cherry Street and just sat in the shade for a bit, watching the city move around me. That's when tax season feels less suffocating—when I'm not at my desk.

The afternoon blurred into more spreadsheets and follow-ups. By the time I drove home, the strike was over and traffic had eased. After a quick dinner, I headed to the pottery studio. There's something about centering clay that makes the numbers and deadlines fade. My hands remembered what my mind needed.
thought_process: The transit strike actually worked in my favor today—no guilt about driving, no standing around waiting. I leaned into that. Tax season has me desk-bound most days, so I was deliberate about the small breaks: that coffee ritual, the food truck lunch, sitting outside. Work was relentless but manageable. The real shift came at the pottery studio. After hours of reconciling other people's finances, I needed to make something with my hands that wasn't about precision or accuracy. That's when I feel most like myself, not the numbers guy. The drive home felt like a gentle descent from the day's intensity.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
